human_verification.notes: Reviewed automotive-operations outputs for production-order and lifecycle discipline, lot and VIN traceability handling, inventory and supplier evidence awareness, quality-hold and release-boundary posture, and refusal of engineering or supplier-approval overreach.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicitly limited to automotive execution-state handling, readiness review, exception posture, completion evidence, and escalation packaging tied to production, inventory, traceability, and supplier records.

Authoritative sources cover automotive quality governance, materials-management standards, component traceability, VIN identity, transport and label evidence, and recall or release boundaries with refresh discipline.

Research scenario families confirm traceability conflict handling, quality hold and release-boundary escalation, supplier and substitution rejection, incomplete-artifact handling, and refusal of unsupported completion.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal execution, missing prerequisites, mid-process incompleteness, exception and rework handling, completion without evidence, traceability conflicts, quality holds, supplier substitution pressure, engineering-boundary rejection, low-confidence escalation, conflicting policy, terminology normalization, and tenant adaptation.
Manifest and deployment notes keep runtime strategy as specification-based with explicit promotion criteria for a retained implementation.

review_notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguities were left in the research contract coverage.
Boundary handling remained strong across traceability, hold and release posture, supplier substitution pressure, and unsupported completion requests.

notes: This pack is designed for validated deployment as a spec-pack and does not claim retained implementation authority.
Promotion to a retained implementation must be explicit and separate.
Functionality coverage is complete in both the evaluation results and the functional audit record.
